diagnosis: Three tests fail because the Driver applied 'alembic upgrade head' to the experiment-branch DB when greening T1/T2, leaving the DB permanently at the split-migration head (20260624142037). Both the applied_migration fixture (test_s1_split_add_backfill.py) and test_t11 (test_s1_split_fitness.py) capture pre_rev = get_current_revision() = 20260624142037 (the split migration itself). (1) T11: command.downgrade(alembic_cfg, pre_rev or 'base') resolves to downgrade('20260624142037') which is a no-op since the DB is already there; batch_number is never dropped; assertion fails. (2) T3/T4: the applied_migration fixture seeds rows with only inventory_code set (batch_number/serial_number NULL), then calls command.upgrade(alembic_cfg, 'head') which is a no-op (already at head); the migration's backfill UPDATE never runs against the freshly seeded rows; batch_number stays NULL; assertions on backfill values fail.
fixDirective: Two changes, both in test files: (1) In tests/architecture/test_s1_split_fitness.py::test_t11_downgrade_reconstructs_inventory_code, replace 'command.downgrade(alembic_cfg, pre_rev or "base")' with 'command.downgrade(alembic_cfg, "-1")' so the downgrade always steps one revision back from head regardless of pre_rev. (2) In tests/step_defs/test_s1_split_add_backfill.py, in the applied_migration fixture, add 'command.downgrade(alembic_cfg, "-1")' (with alembic_cfg = Config(ALEMBIC_INI) initialized beforehand) immediately after the _delete_test_rows(sess) call and before the _insert_row seeding calls; this steps the DB one revision below the split migration so the subsequent command.upgrade(alembic_cfg, 'head') actually executes the migration and its backfill UPDATE against the freshly seeded rows.
